interface: Vault HTTP API v1 — KV secrets engine version 2 (stable)

request_headers.X-Vault-Token: the client token, on every request
request_headers.X-Vault-Namespace: namespace path such as team-eng/ — sent only when a namespace is configured; requests are routed relative to it. A root-namespace client must omit the header entirely.
request_headers.Content-Type: application/json on requests that carry a body

paths.rule: KV v2 endpoints are mount-scoped: the mount name is a URL segment and the operation segment (data / delete / undelete / destroy / metadata) sits between the mount and the secret path

read_secret.query: ?version=N selects a specific version; a latest-version read sends no version parameter at all

metadata.deletion_time: RFC3339 string, empty when not deleted

read_secret.deleted_or_destroyed_version: HTTP 404 whose body still carries a data object: data.data is null and data.metadata shows a non-empty deletion_time (soft-deleted) or destroyed=true. The client returns the metadata with a nil payload instead of treating this as an error.
read_secret.missing_secret: HTTP 404 with {"errors":[]} — an empty errors array; mapped to the package's not-found sentinel

cas_rules: the options object is omitted entirely when no check-and-set is requested
cas is a JSON number; cas=0 means the write is allowed only if the key does not yet exist
cas=N (N>0) means the write is allowed only if N matches the key's current version

errors: check-and-set parameter did not match the current version

cas_conflict.note: deterministic fixture message used by the fake server; detection matches 400 plus a check-and-set error string

destroy_versions.note: destroy is documented with PUT, unlike delete/undelete which are documented with POST

success_body_fields.versions: object keyed by decimal version-number strings; each value has created_time / deletion_time / destroyed but no version field — the client fills the version from the map key

rules: any non-2xx response with a non-empty errors array decodes into a structured API error preserving the HTTP status code and every message
404 with an empty errors array means the path does not exist OR the token is not permitted to read it; Vault keeps that ambiguity intentionally
404 with a data object on the data endpoint is NOT an error document — it is a deleted/destroyed version read (see read_secret)
